text:
REGIME — Defensive rotation, metals surged, software lagged, breadth mixed.

INDICES — VIX 25.3, falling. IWM +1.22%, QQQ +0.66%, DIA +0.64%, SPY +0.56%; small caps outperformed large caps across the board. Silver surged +3.59%, the standout cross-asset move, with gold +3.01% as inflation hedges dominated.

SECTORS — XLB +1.98%, XLV +1.00%, XLY +0.96%, XLI +0.67%, XLP +0.49%, XLK +0.45%, XLU +0.35%, XLC +0.28%, XLF +0.12%, XLRE -0.05%, XLE -0.44%. Top industries: REMX +5.35% and WGMI +3.68%; bottom: MSOS -1.32% and BOAT -1.26%.

THE 7s — Semi +3.64%, New Metals +2.12%, Old Metals +2.03%, Defense +1.12%, Health +1.09%, Buildout +1.03%, Housing +0.94%, Old Staples +0.78%, Freight +0.78%, Bank +0.70%, Industrial +0.65%, Cloud +0.58%, New Staples +0.45%, Power +0.40%, Medtech +0.36%, Energy +0.08%, Insurance -0.15%, Toll Road -0.22%, Fab -0.92%, Software -1.17%. Strongest ticker in Semi 7 not individually named in data; weakest basket Software 7's drag tied to macro rate pressure.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 51.5, Neutral. Top movers: REMX +5.35% (Industries), JEF +10%+ (Financials), SLV +3.59% (Cross-Asset). 20d momentum: best sector XLE +10.39%, worst XLP -8.43%; best basket Energy +13.13%, worst Old Metals -19.17%. JEF's 10%+ spike was driven by buyout rumors involving Japan's SMFG, a direct M&A catalyst in financials.

WATCH — IJK (vol 16.9x, +1.02%): extreme volume spike on mid-cap value warrants follow-through check. REMX (+5.35%): rare metals ETF momentum diverging sharply from 20d trend, watch for reversal. XLE (-0.44%): energy lagged despite oil above $100, trend divergence from 20d +10.39% worth monitoring.
